differential_diagnoses: Metastatic occult malignancy (Carcinomatosis) with bone marrow infiltration
Occult hematologic malignancy (e.g., myelodysplastic syndrome or myeloid sarcoma)
Disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC) secondary to malignancy
Severe systemic infection (e.g., disseminated tuberculosis or fungal infection)
next_steps: Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and cultures
Perform HScore calculation and formal HLH diagnostic evaluation (soluble CD25, NK cell activity)
Computed tomography of the chest with contrast to evaluate pulmonary nodules
Repeat coagulation profile (fibrinogen, D-dimer) to assess for ongoing DIC
Liver biopsy to determine the nature of hepatic nodularity
Transition to targeted symptom management and initiate goals-of-care discussions
